tasks.title: Extract comparison and history workspace ownership out of App.tsx
tasks.description: Move the remaining comparison/history and run-panel state or rendering bodies behind route-owned workspace modules so `App.tsx` keeps shell composition only while existing route semantics, barrels, and smoke-test coverage stay intact.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply says the next hexagonal split should keep the import surface stable while moving ownership into feature-owned modules, and the frontend target/current-state docs call out comparison/history and run-panel rendering as the next waves still concentrated in App.tsx.
tasks.priority_reason: This is the clearest immediate continuation because App.tsx remains the largest active pressure point, route smoke tests already exist as the floor for this extraction, and the direction explicitly prioritizes route-owned workspace ownership ahead of adding more logic to the giant entrypoint.

tasks.title: Move guarded-live and provider-governance orchestration out of application.py
tasks.description: Keep `application.py` import-compatible but peel guarded-live and provider-governance orchestration into bounded `application_support/*` or nearby use-case modules so the compatibility facade stops accumulating policy and serializer logic outside the already-split comparison path.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The backend target and current-state docs both say comparison extraction is real now, but guarded-live and provider-governance orchestration still need the same treatment in application.py to satisfy the compatibility-facade goal.
tasks.priority_reason: This remains a direct follow-up in the same lane, but it should stay proposed behind the App.tsx route-owned split so the queue keeps one deterministic next task while frontend ownership extraction continues to unblock further bounded-flow moves.

tasks.title: Split operator and guarded-live model families out of domain/models.py
tasks.description: Continue the `domain/models.py` compatibility re-export pattern by extracting the remaining operator and guarded-live model families into `domain/model_types/*` modules so new backend feature work no longer defaults to the broad shared model file.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The direction detail and current-state docs explicitly note that provider-provenance records already moved behind domain/models.py compatibility imports, while the remaining operator and guarded-live families still need the same bounded-family treatment.
tasks.priority_reason: This is still clearly executable work, but it stays proposed because the immediate next continuation from the latest reply is to identify and execute the highest-pressure hexagonal split candidate first rather than starting multiple large decomposition fronts at once.
